title: LangChain Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using LangChain for AI tool development.

commands.description: Import the PromptTemplate class for creating structured prompts.
commands.scenario: When you need to create a custom prompt template for a specific task.

commands.description: Initialize an LLMChain with a language model and prompt.
commands.scenario: To set up a chain for processing prompts with a language model.

commands.description: Enable verbose mode to get detailed logs of operations.
commands.scenario: When debugging or trying to understand the flow of execution.

commands.description: Execute the LLMChain with input data to get the output.
commands.scenario: When processing input data through the chain to generate results.

commands.description: Create a memory buffer to store conversation history.
commands.scenario: When building a chatbot that requires context retention.

commands.description: Initialize an agent with tools and a language model for zero-shot tasks.
commands.scenario: For creating an agent capable of handling tasks without prior examples.

commands.description: Define a tool with a specific function for the agent to use.
commands.scenario: When adding custom functionality to an agent.

commands.description: Specify a configuration file for setting up the environment.
commands.scenario: To load environment settings from a predefined YAML file.

commands.description: Create a prompt template for translation tasks.
commands.scenario: When designing a prompt for translating text between languages.

commands.description: Initialize an OpenAI language model with a specific version.
commands.scenario: To use a specific OpenAI model for language processing tasks.

commands.description: Set up an executor to run an agent with its tools.
commands.scenario: When you need to execute tasks using an agent and its tools.

commands.description: Disable streaming mode for non-streaming output.
commands.scenario: When you need to process outputs in a batch mode instead of streaming.

commands.description: Attach a memory component to an LLMChain for stateful interactions.
commands.scenario: When you want to maintain state across multiple interactions in a chain.

commands.description: Execute an agent with given input data to perform tasks.
commands.scenario: To perform tasks using an agent with the provided input.

commands.description: Use the LangSmith callback handler to enable deep-trace debugging and observability for complex chain execution flows.
Implement LCEL (LangChain Expression Language) for modular, composable, and asynchronous chain construction instead of legacy LLMChain.
Use the .bind() method to pass specific stop sequences or model-specific parameters (like temperature or top_p) directly to the underlying LLM during chain execution.
Optimize retrieval quality by using Maximum Marginal Relevance (MMR) to reduce redundancy in retrieved document chunks.
Utilize asynchronous streaming to reduce perceived latency by yielding tokens as they are generated rather than waiting for the full response.
Pass custom callback handlers (like LangSmith or custom logging) at runtime to a specific chain execution without modifying the chain definition.
The modern way to attach tool definitions directly to an LLM object, enabling native function calling capabilities for models like GPT-4.
Used within LCEL to add new keys to the input dictionary while preserving existing ones, essential for complex data flow in chains.
Filters retrieved documents based on a minimum similarity score rather than just returning the top 'k' results, reducing noise in RAG pipelines.
Executes multiple inputs through a chain in parallel using asyncio, significantly improving throughput compared to sequential loops.
